creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Garrett Galow from WorkOS presented Studio, an internal workspace that allows non-technical users to query Snowflake, Linear, and Notion using natural language. The LLM generates reusable, deterministic JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, with reliability ensured through preflight sequencing, schema context layering, and query validation.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google Chrome that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tools instead of DOM automation. It provides both declarative APIs using HTML attributes and imperative APIs for custom JavaScript tools, allowing agents to call named typed actions directly rather than relying on pixel-based clicking and layout interpretation.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser demonstrates a workflow architecture that dispatches AI agents to perform development tasks in isolated environments while the human is away, using signal agents to monitor Slack and Linear, verification gates spanning lint through critic passes, weekly JSONL analysis for inefficiency detection, and physiological monitoring via Oura ring over MCP. The core insight is that human attention, not tool speed, is the constraint.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford presents an approach to training smaller models with reinforcement learning to improve tool use and self-correction capabilities. A 4B model fine-tuned with RL to use the get_table_name function outperformed the much larger Qwen 3 235B on a database query task, achieving self-correction and accurate results at under $500 training cost.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Building with Anthropic's Fable 5 LIVE - Is It Really Worth the Hype?
videos.summary: Cole Medin tests Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model against real-world tasks to assess whether its capabilities justify the 2x price premium over Claude Opus 4.8. The stream evaluates whether the claimed increases in model capability deliver practical value.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open source command-line tool that enables rapid development and production deployment of AI agents with built-in identity management, code sandboxing, and audit trails. The tool integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude and simplifies the deployment process compared to traditional agent frameworks.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin announces a live AI Q&A session scheduled for Saturday mornings where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, and related tools. The session will also feature casual chess gameplay and the host will answer questions spanning Claude Code, Archon, and other AI development topics.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a multi-model workflow where Claude Opus handles reasoning and planning while Gemini 3.5 Flash specializes in frontend UI design. The approach routes each development phase to the model best suited for it, improving output quality and cost efficiency compared to using a single model throughout.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: A video explaining how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems through autonomous loops. Covers proactive AI loop architecture, use cases in autonomous ads optimization and SEO, and tools like Loopany that enable memory and tool integration for agent autonomy.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding tools and features, presenting a perspective on next-generation code generation capabilities. The video covers /goal feature mechanics, setup, and prompt engineering for autonomous tasks, as well as tools like Crewlet and Superdesign.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is presented as a new AI coding paradigm. The video covers how Symphony works, its harness setup with auto testing capabilities, and WORKFLOW.md integration for Symphony setup.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: An overview video comparing AI agent tools and architectures, including autoresearch and autoagent capabilities, Claude Code's 3-layer memory system, and self-evolving mechanisms in Hermes and Openclaw. The video highlights practical agent frameworks for building autonomous systems.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series of models in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer, Latent Mixture of Experts, and Multi-Token Prediction architectures.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model that shifts from full attention to sparse attention via MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA). The model includes tiled and I/O optimizations for KV cache, reducing latency in both prefill and decode inference stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is described as a unique agentic framework that offers novel ways to harness and deploy AI capabilities. The video compares Pi's framework architecture against competing agent products like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity in terms of harness complexity and feature richness.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Huawei announced the Tau Scaling Law to compete in the hardware and semiconductor race. The industry is focusing on EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding as key technological areas driving competition between China and the USA.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses strategies for tech job hunting in 2026 amid AI screening and automation. The video covers personal branding, networking value, and argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI advances. It emphasizes that jobs evolve rather than disappear, and recommends hands-on learning through open source contributions and building projects.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: Five production deployment practices for AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with API Gateway and IAM separation, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost budgets via lifecycle hooks and model routing, and observability via metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: Educational video demonstrating Infrastructure as Code for building a serverless REST API on AWS. The tutorial covers AWS CDK with Python, showing both manual AWS console setup and automated CDK deployment of DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway resources.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS video demonstrates five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations and token waste in agentic systems. Semantic tool selection filters context windows, graph RAG provides computed answers for precise queries, multi-agent validation catches failures, symbolic guardrails enforce rules in code, and runtime guardrails enable agent self-correction.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses whether Google engineers use AI-assisted code generation ("vibing code") compared to OpenAI Codex and Claude Code. The video spans Google's practices from 2024 through 2026 projections, including analysis of their internal tools and approach to AI-assisted development.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool positioned as disruptive to open source models. The video explores how Malus works, its implications for open source maintainers, and debates whether the business is harmful to the open source ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: A YouTube video documenting an experiment where Claude Code was tasked with completing a developer's work, demonstrating its performance on coding tasks including terminal operations and workflow automation.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses how to identify genuine product-market fit versus founder delusion. He argues that rabid customer enthusiasm and formal interviews are unreliable signals, and that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than push. He advocates embedding in customer workflows over structured interviews and betting on where AI models are heading.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, raised $26M for an ad monetization platform targeting AI tools. He argues that ads rather than subscriptions are the viable path forward for AI applications, and identifies human trust as the primary bottleneck for AI agent adoption, not technological capability.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapped path to $10M ARR with no external funding. The interview covers growth milestones including hitting $1M ARR in 117 days, churn reduction strategies, pricing experiments, SEO/AEO tactics, and decision frameworks for solo vs co-founder models and fundraising timing.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh, an Associate Professor at CU Boulder, argues that teaching AI through manual, slow-paced learning focused on mathematical foundations produces knowledge that outlasts technology hype cycles. He critiques the "AI-native" mindset and advocates learning core concepts by hand rather than relying on the latest tools.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Agent Loop is Here. And it Changes Everything - Here's How.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses the implications of AI agent loops, comparing Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models and examining capabilities including recursive self-improvement, long-running task memory, and agentic payments. The video covers loop engineering patterns, price elasticity in AI, and emerging builder opportunities in regulated industries.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
